response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer with genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS app, FOSS4G talk) and some distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust in a production navigation/routing context. His C++ work is limited to a 2014–2015 stint writing IDE components at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis audio project — neither touches routing tiling or geospatial pipelines. His entire career arc since 2015 runs firmly through TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of Rust and no navigation-guidance or routing-data-pipeline work at any level.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map) with a conference talk at FOSS4G 2019, demonstrating domain familiarity
Distributed systems and high-throughput infrastructure background at Dunnhumby: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O
Strong quality-assurance culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev) and ability to work across multiple layers of the stack
concerns: No production C++ or Rust experience in the past decade; last C++ work (2014–2015) was unrelated to geospatial or routing domains
Zero experience with routing data pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or routing tile generation — the defining technical scope of this role
Current and recent trajectory (Stripe, Shopify) is squarely TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, representing a significant mismatch with the systems-programming focus required
